MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Andrew M. Purdy for Petitioner Ryan McLaughlin.
Ethan Jacobs for Movant John/Jane Doe.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PETITIONER JOHN/JANE DOE's Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena Duces Tecum And For Monetary Sanctions is under submission.

Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR#5041, email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. =(302/RBU)

Petitioner John/Jane Doe'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena Duces Tecum And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-06-2019 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: Petitioner John/Jane Doe's Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena Duces Tecum and For Monetary Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: As a motion to quash appears to remain pending in the Illinois court, John/Jane Doe's motion to quash and for monetary sanctions in our court is ordered off calendar until such time as the parties' notify our court that the Illinois court has ruled. This is at base an Illinois matter and we defer to the Illinois court.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano, CSR#8600, e-mail: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

Petitioner John/Jane Doe'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena Duces Tecum And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-28-2019 Continued To Sep-06-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Petitioner John/Jane Doe'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena Duces Tecum And For Monetary Sanctions:

Petitioner John/Jane Doe's motion to quash subpoena duces tecum and for sanctions is CONTINUED to September 6, 2019 at 9:30 AM in Department 302 to allow the Illinois court to rule on the motions to quash and intervene that are pending before it. Movant's status update indicates that the hearing in Illinois has been canceled, but that it is expected to be reset some time in August 2019. The parties are instructed to advise this court of the Illinois court's rulings.
